original_abstract: In this paper, we introduce the Adaptive Recursive Neural Network (AdaRNN) for target-dependent sentiment classification on Twitter. Unlike traditional models, AdaRNN adaptively propagates sentiment signals from individual words to a specific target by leveraging both contextual nuances and syntactic relationships. The architecture employs multiple composition functions, modeling the sentiment propagation process as a dynamic distribution over these functions to better capture complex linguistic patterns. Our experimental results demonstrate that AdaRNN significantly outperforms existing baseline methods. Additionally, we contribute to the research community by introducing a new, manually annotated dataset specifically designed for target-dependent sentiment analysis in the Twitter domain.
humanized_abstract: In this work, we present the Adaptive Recursive Neural Network (AdaRNN) approach designed for the task of target-dependent sentiment classification on Twitter. Unlike previous approaches, the AdaRNN not only utilizes the contextual nuances of the words in relation to the target and the sentiment they are expressing but also the syntactic relationships between the words and the target. We model the composition of these sentiment relations using multiple functions and a dynamic distribution over these functions that is capable of capturing diverse linguistic constructs. Finally, we evaluate the performance of AdaRNN against some strong baselines and we demonstrate state of the art performance on the task. We also introduce a new dataset, annotated manually and designed to specifically investigate the task of target-dependent sentiment analysis on Twitter.
